spec_text:
Backoffice

Requirements Mapping page

View list of requriements mapping pairs

Do we need it for Process Requirements?

User with access to PICASso content in Backoffice should see new page "Requirements Mapping" under the "Product Requirements" section with the following content:

Filters:
- Search field - allowing to search by Requirement Code
- Standard - dropdown, allowing to select all standards available in the system
- Linked Standard - dropdown, allowing to select all standards available in the system
- Show Inactive - toggle button:
  * When unchecked (default) - only active mappings are visible in the list
  * When checked - both active and inactive mapping are shown

Table with following columns with the mapping between requirements of different standard:
- Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
- Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement. Note: value in this column is clickable and should redirect the user to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly.
- Linked Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
- Linked Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement. Note: value in this column is clickable and should redirect the user to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly.
- Is Active - showing status of the mapping pair:
  * Green checkmark - when active
  * Grey checkmark - when inactive
- Actions - three dots menu button with following options:
  * Edit - allowing to edit the mapping pair which is already added
  * Activate (or Deactivate) - allowing to activate or deactivate the mapping pair depending on the current status of the pair.

Add/edit requirements mapping pair

On the "Requirements Mapping" page the user can add new requriements mapping pair or edit existing one by:
- clicking the button "Add Requirements Mapping Pair" - "New Requirement Mapping Pair" page is opened.
- clicking the menu button in the column "Actions" for the requirements mappig pair and selecting option "Edit" - "Edit Requirement Mapping Pair" page is opened.

These pages should have the following fields:

Standard: Dropdown, single select, mandatory - Allowing to select the standard of all the standards already available in the system
Requirement: Dropdown, multi select, mandatory - Activated after specific standard is selected, allowing to select requirements/sub-requirements which are related to this selected standard only.
Linked Standard: Dropdown with possibility to type, mandatory - Allowing to add new standard or select the name of all the standards already available in the system (if any were already created)
Linked Requirement: Dropdown, multi select, mandatory - Activated after specific standard is selected, allowing to select requirements/sub-requirements which are related to this selected linked standard only.
Is active: Checkbox - Allowing to update the status of requirements mapping pair from this screen: When checked (default) - the mapping is considered as active. When unchecked - the mapping is considered inactive

After the data is entered, the user can click on the button "Save" and the system should validate if all the mandatory fields are filled in:
- If at least 1 is empty - the user should see an error "Please fill in the required fields" at the top of the screen and corresponding empty field highlighted in red with error message "Required Field!".
- If all are filled in - the user should see:
  * Success message at the top of the screen with the text "Requirement mapping pair is successfully added." or "Requirement mapping pair is successfully updated."
  * The user is redirected to the Requirements Mapping page.
  * And this new/updated mapping pair is updated in all the versions of the requirements selected in this mapping pair.
  * Note: if with edit previous requirements were removed - they should be removed from all the versions of requirements as well.

The user can also click on the "Back" button on the "New Requirement Mapping Pair" or "Edit Requirement Mapping Pair" page and no new mapping pair or no changes would be saved.

Activating/deactivating requirements mapping pair

If the requirements mapping pair is active, user can select the option "Deactivate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Requirements Mapping" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to deactivate?" and 2 options:
- Cancel - closes the pop-up and requirements mapping pair remains active
- Ok - closes the pop-up and deactivates the pair:
  * success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Requirements pair <Requirement> to <Linked requirement> is successfully deactivated".
  * status of the requirements mapping pair is updated to "Inactive".
  * mapping pair is not considered for the view in release, when specific view is selected for the release.

If the requirements mapping pair is inactive, user can select the option "Activate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Requirements Mapping" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to activate?" and 2 options:
- Cancel - closes the pop-up and requirements mapping pair remains inactive
- Ok - closes the pop-up and activates the pair:
  * success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Requirements pair <Requirement> to <Linked requirement> is successfully activated".
  * status of the requirements mapping pair is updated to "Active".
  * mapping pair is no considered for the view in release, when specific view is selected for the release.

Updating requirements mapping with the file upload

On the "Requirements Mapping" page the user should be able to download the list of the Requirements Mapping pairs available in the system by clicking the button "Download Requirements Mapping" and the file with the list of available mapping pairs would be exported with the following columns:
- Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
- Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement (several values separated by comma)
- Linked Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
- Linked Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement (several values separated by comma)
- Is Active - showing status of the mapping pair:
  * True - when active
  * False - when inactive

The user should be also able to download empty template file by clicking the button "Template Download" and empty file with the same set of columns would be exported:
- Standard (string)
- Requirement (string, several values separated by comma)
- Linked Standard (string)
- Linked Requirement (string, several values separated by comma)
- Is Active (boolean (True or False), empty value is considered as "True".

This downloaded template user can fill-in and upload back to the system using "Select file" button. Upon uploading the file - the following validations should be checked

Validation table:
- Standard: Value is empty - "Standard column can't be empty Lines <line number>"
- Standard: Value specified does not correspond to any standards registered in the system - "Standard does not exist in the system Lines <line number>"
- Requirement: Value is empty - "Requirement column can't be empty Lines <line number>"
- Requirement: Value specified does not correspond to any requirement/sub-requriement registered in the system or existing requirements are divided by other symbol (not comma) - "Requirement does not exist in the system. Several codes can be separated only by comma. Lines <line number>"
- Linked Standard: Value is empty - "Linked standard column can't be empty Lines <line number>"
- Linked Standard: Value specified does not correspond to any standards registered in the system - "Linked standard does not exist in the system Lines <line number>"
- Linked Requirement: Value is empty - "Linked requirement column can't be empty Lines <line number>"
- Linked Requirement: Value specified does not correspond to any requirement/sub-requriement registered in the system or existing requirements are divided by other symbol (not comma) - "Linked requirement does not exist in the system. Several codes can be separated only by comma. Lines <line number>"
- Is Active: Invalid data is specified (other than "True", "False" or empty) - "Is Active status contains unsupported value (can be only True or False) Lines <line number>"
- Wrong file format - "Please upload valid file type (.xlsx)"
- File structure does not correspond to the set of columns in the template file - "Columns structure is incorrect, please use the columns from the template file."

If all the validations are successfulluy passed, the user should see:
- Success message at the top of the screen with the text "File was uploaded successfully"
- New/updated mapping pairs are updated in all the versions of the requirements selected in this mapping pair.
- Note: if with edit previous requirements were removed - they should be removed from all the versions of requirements as well.

Requirements Mapping tab on the Edit Requirements page

In question if we need it

New tab "Requriements Mapping" should be added for the "Edit Product Requirement/Sub-requirement" page where all the standards and requirements linked with this requirement should be shown in the following content:

Filters:
- Search field - allowing to search by Requirement Code
- Standard - dropdown, allowing to select all standards available in the system
- Show Inactive - toggle button:
  * When unchecked (default) - only active mappings are visible in the list
  * When checked - both active and inactive mapping are shown

Table with following columns with the mapping between requirements of different standard:
- Linked Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
- Linked Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement. Note: value in this column is clickable and should redirect the user to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly.
- Is Active - showing status of the mapping pair:
  * Green checkmark - when active
  * Grey checkmark - when inactive

"Standard" page for Product Requirements

View list of standards

User with access to PICASso content in Backoffice should see new page "Standards" under the "Product Requirements" section with the following content:

Filters:
- Search field - allowing to search by Standard Name/Code
- Show Inactive - toggle button:
  * When unchecked (default) - only active standards are visible in the list
  * When checked - both active and inactive standards are shown

Table with following columns:
- Code - unique code of the standard
- Standard - name of the standard/policy related to the requirement. Note: the value in this column is clickable and opens "Edit Standard" page
- Is Active - showing status of standard:
  * Green checkmark - when active
  * Grey checkmark - when inactive
- Actions - three dots menu button with following options:
  * Edit - allowing to edit standard which is already added
  * Activate (or Deactivate) - allowing to activate or deactivate standard depending on its current status.

Add/edit standard

On the "Standards" page the user can add new standard or edit existing by:
- clicking the button "Add Standard" - "New Standard" page is opened.
- clicking on the standard's name or clicking the menu button in the column "Actions" and selecting option "Edit" - "Edit Standard" page is opened.

These pages should have the following fields:

Code: String, mandatory - Allowing to type unique code for the standard
Standard: String, mandatory - Allowing to type name of the standard that would be shown for configurations
Is active: Checkbox - Allowing to update the status of standard from this screen: When checked (default) - standard is considered as active. When unchecked - standard is considered inactive

After the data is entered, the user can click on the button "Save" and the system should validate the following points:
- If specified Code is already used for other standard already created - the user should see an error "Specified Code is already used for other standard. Please check and update the value" at the top of the screen and corresponding Code field highlighted in red with error message "Code should be unique".
- If at least 1 mandatory field is empty - the user should see an error "Please fill in the required fields" at the top of the screen and corresponding empty field highlighted in red with error message "Required Field!".
- If above validations are passed - the user should see:
  * Success message at the top of the screen with the text "Standard is successfully added." or "Standard is successfully updated."
  * The user is redirected to the Standards page.
  * Corresponding standard is updated for the Sources page right after the change is saved.

The user can also click on the "Back" button on the "New Standard" or "Edit Standard" page and no new standard or no changes would be saved.

Activating/deactivating standard

If the standard is active, user can select the option "Deactivate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Standards" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to deactivate?" and 2 options:
- Cancel - closes the pop-up and standard remains active
- Ok - closes the pop-up and deactivates the standard:
  * success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Standard <Standard name> is successfully deactivated".
  * status of the standard is updated to "Inactive".
  * standard cannot be selected for the Sources when they are edited.

If the standard is inactive, user can select the option "Activate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Standards" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to activate?" and 2 options:
- Cancel - closes the pop-up and standard remains inactive
- Ok - closes the pop-up and activates the standard:
  * success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Standard <Standard name> is successfully activated".
  * status of the standard is updated to "Active".
  * standard can be selected for the Sources when they are edited.

Add "Standard" field for "Requriements Sources" page

Add new column "Standard" for the "Source List" page (Backoffice → Product Requirements → Source) with the data from the corresponding "Standard" field of the Source (string). Remove irrelevant columns "Is Security Level" and "Is Regional".

Add new optional dropdown field "Standard" to the pages "New Source" and "Edit Source", which should allow the user to select standard available in PICASso (active in the Stadards LoV). Remove irrelevant fields "Is Security Level" and "Is Regional".

"Priority" page for Product Requirements

View list of priorities

User with access to PICASso content in Backoffice should see new page "Priorities" under the "Product Requirements" section with the following content:

Filters:
- Search field - allowing to search by Priority Name/Code
- Show Inactive - toggle button:
  * When unchecked (default) - only active priorities are visible in the list
  * When checked - both active and inactive priorities are shown

Table with following columns:
- Code - unique code of the priority
- Priority - name of the priority of the requirement. Note: the value in this column is clickable and opens "Edit Priority" page
- Is Active - showing status of priority:
  * Green checkmark - when active
  * Grey checkmark - when inactive
- Actions - three dots menu button with following options:
  * Edit - allowing to edit priority which is already added
  * Activate (or Deactivate) - allowing to activate or deactivate priority depending on its current status.

Add/edit priority

On the "Priorities" page the user can add new priorities or edit existing by:
- clicking the button "Add Priority" - "New Standard" page is opened.
- clicking on the priority's name or clicking the menu button in the column "Actions" and selecting option "Edit" - "Edit Priority" page is opened.

These pages should have the following fields:

Code: String, mandatory - Allowing to type unique code for the priority
Priority: String, mandatory - Allowing to type name of the priority that would be shown for configurations
Is active: Checkbox - Allowing to update the status of priority from this screen: When checked (default) -priority is considered as active. When unchecked -priority is considered inactive

After the data is entered, the user can click on the button "Save" and the system should validate the following points:
- If specified Code is already used for other priority already created - the user should see an error "Specified Code is already used for other priority. Please check and update the value" at the top of the screen and corresponding Code field highlighted in red with error message "Code should be unique".
- If at least 1 mandatory field is empty - the user should see an error "Please fill in the required fields" at the top of the screen and corresponding empty field highlighted in red with error message "Required Field!".
- If above validations are passed - the user should see:
  * Success message at the top of the screen with the text "Priority is successfully added." or "Priority is successfully updated."
  * The user is redirected to the Priorities page.
  * Corresponding priority is updated for the respective Product Requirement/sub-requirement (if it was already added for any requirement).

The user can also click on the "Back" button on the "New Priority" or "Edit Priority" pages and no new priority or no changes would be saved.

Activating/deactivating priority

If the priority is active, user can select the option "Deactivate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Priorities" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to deactivate?" and 2 options:
- Cancel - closes the pop-up and priority remains active
- Ok - closes the pop-up and deactivates the priority:
  * success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Priority <Priority name> is successfully deactivated".
  * status of the priority is updated to "Inactive".
  * priority cannot be selected for the Product Requirements/Sub-requirements and Custom Requirements when they are edited, but it still remains visible when requirement is opened for view (or when the list of requirements is exported) - both in Backoffice and on Frontoffice.

If the priority is inactive, user can select the option "Activate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Priorities" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to activate?" and 2 options:
- Cancel - closes the pop-up and priority remains inactive
- Ok - closes the pop-up and activates the priority:
  * success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Priority <Priority name> is successfully activated".
  * status of the priority is updated to "Active".
  * priority cann be selected for the Product Requirements/Sub-requirements and Custom Requirements when they are edited.

Update "Must Or Should" field and column across the system to "Priority"

Existing column/field "Must Or Should" should be updated to "Priority" across the system:

Backoffice:
- "New Product Requirement" and "Edit Product Requirement" pages
- "New Product Sub-Requirement" and "Edit Product Sub-Requirement" pages
- Product Requirement export and template file
- Product Sub-Requirement export and template file

Frontoffice:
- "Add Custom Requirement" and "Edit Custom Requirement" pop-ups
- Column on the "Product Requirements" page in the release
- Product Requirement export file in the release

Note: 1) field name in the POST /requirements API used to send requirements to Jama would remain unchanged, but new values added for these requriements on the Priorities page in Backoffice should be sent in this "MustOrShould" field.
2) for the product requriements update in the Backoffice, changes for the "Priority" column/field should be still counted as "Minor" change.

For all existing requirements values which are currently added to the "Must Or Should" column/field should remain, while only name of this column/field should be updated to "Priority". Existing "Must" and "Should" values should be added as 1st values for the new "Priorities" page in the Backoffice.

Frontoffice

Default view of requirements tracebility on Product Details

On the Product Details page the user should see new field "Default Requirements traceability view" on the "Product Configuration sub-tab". This field should allow to select the default perspective to view the data on the Product Requirements page in the releases of this product.

The field is a dropdown field which will have the following options:
- All requirements (default option)
- All the possible combination of standards mapping (as per the configuration in the Backoffice "Requirements Mapping" page)

Example: if in the Backoffice we have the following requirements mapping pairs:

Standard: CTI/CTS, Requirement: CTI042B_004 CTI048_002 CTI048_020 CTI048_021 CTI048_031 CTI048_033, Linked Standard: IEC62443, Linked Requirement: CR 1.1 SR 1.1
Standard: CTI/CTS, Requirement: CTS048_005 CTS048_006, Linked Standard: IEC62443, Linked Requirement: CR 1.1
Standard: IEC62443, Requirement: SR 1.1, Linked Standard: OWASP, Linked Requirement: V6.3.4 V6.3.8
Standard: CTI/CTS, Requirement: CTI042B_004 CTI048_002 CTI048_020 CTI048_021 CTI048_031 CTI048_033, Linked Standard: OWASP, Linked Requirement: V6.3.4 V6.3.8

For the field "Default Requirements traceability view" we will have the following options:
- All Requirements
- CTI/CTS -> IEC62443
- IEC62443 -> CTI/CTS
- IEC62443 -> OWASP
- OWASP -> IEC62443
- CTI/CTS -> OWASP
- OWASP -> CTI/CTS

Option selected for the field "Default Requirements traceability view" on the product would show the list of Product Requriements in all the releases of this product with this tracebility view already selected.

For all existing products this field should be added with "Default Requirements traceability view" = "All requirements".

Updating buttons to icons on Product Requirements and Process Requirements pages

When user opens Product Requriements and Process requirements page in the release, he should see the buttons and toggles displayed on the page were updated to the icons shown in the right corner over the table header:
- Refresh Jama (or Jira) - this icon is shown only when requirements were submitted to Jama/Jira with respective "Last update from Jira <date and time>"/"Last update from Jama <date and time>" message. And respective error icon and hover message (if the data were not updated due to an error).
- Diagrams icon - opens the "Requirement Status Summary" pop-up
- Settings pop-up - shows toggles available for the page:
  * Process requirements:
    + Show Sub-Requirements (only if they are visible on the page) - expand all sub-requirements on the page
    + Show All Requirements - show all (even not scopped) requirements
  * Product requirements:
    + Show Sub-Requirements - expand all sub-requirements on the page
    + Show All Requirements - show all (even not scopped) requirements
    + Tracebility view (drop-down) - allow to select the perspective to view the list of the Product Requirements in this release.
- Import/Export icon - upon clicking, showing 2 options:
  * Import XLSX - allowing to import file to update requirements statuses or create new custom requirements (only shown during Scoping, Scope Approval and Manage stages). Note: import progress and link to the error pop-up would be shown under the icon.
  * Export XLSX - allowing to export file with the list of requirements, their statuses and delegation details. Note: for users with view privilege and on the stages where requirement's status cannot be edited, upon clicking on this icon - export of the file should be started.

Selecting/changing Requirements Tracebility view in Release

Upon clicking on the "Options" icon on the Product Requirements page, the user should see dropdown field "Traceability view" where all active standards mapping pairs available in PICASso can be selected.

Upon selecting specific mapping pair from the list, the user should see that requirements list view is refreshed and linked requirements from the selected mapping pair are shown connected on the Product Requirements page in the following hierarchy:

1. Parent of the requirement from 1st Standard in selected the pair
2. Requirement linked to the parent requriement from 2nd Standard in selected the pair (if any) - marked in blue with the chain icon in front of it
3. Sub-requirement related to this parent from 1st Standard in selected the pair
4. Requirement linked to this sub-requriement from 2nd Standard in selected the pair (if any) - marked in blue with the chain icon in front of it

Example: If user has selected Tracebility view = "IEC62443 -> CTI/CTS", than "IEC62443" is considered 1st standard and "CTI/CTS" - 2nd. But If user has selected Tracebility view = "CTI/CTS -> IEC62443", than "CTI/CTS" is the 1st standard and "IEC62443" - 2nd.

Note: 1) Category of the linked requriement should be ignored.
2) If linked requirement is not scoped for the release - it should be shown on the page, only when "Show All Requirements" toggle is activated or when this requirement is added manually to release.

All requirements not related to selected mapping pair should be still shown on the page. "Traceability view" selected should be treated as updated display, not as the filter.

When specific "Traceability view" is selected the user should see "Tracebility focus" filter with 3 options:
- All requirements (default) - both mapped and not mapped would be shown.
- Only gaps (not linked) - only requirements which are not linked using this selected mapping pair would be shown.
- Only linked - only requirements which are mapped using this selected mapping pair would be shown.

Adding "Priority" filter for the product requirements in release

The user should see new filter "Priority" on the "Product Requirements" page and on the "Product Requirement Summary" tab on the "Cybersecurity Residual Risks" page.

"Priority" column should be also added to the product requirements table on the "Product Requirement Summary" tab on the "Cybersecurity Residual Risks" page.

Mapped requirements display for Requirements Evaluation

Do we need this view here?

Upon clicking on the "Options" icon on the "Product Requirement Summary" tab on the "Cybersecurity Residual Risks" page, the user should see dropdown field "Traceability view" where all active standards mapping pairs available in PICASso can be selected. The behavior mirrors the Product Requirements page traceability view.
